commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The PR needs contributor live-behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the data-at-rest/session-state boundary before any merge path; the narrow code finding should be fixed on the PR branch.; Needs attention: The diff has no supply-chain change, but it deliberately adds a sensitive local queue-state file that maintainers should review as a security-boundary change.; Review finding: [P2] Persist aborted-drop ackn... (sha=f084e50fe9052b354f64d08bb220a31e2f3f4cdc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds JSON persistence/restoration for auto-reply followup queues, persists queue mutations, restores queues on module initialization, and extends queue drain/persistence tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main stores FOLLOWUP_QUEUES only in a process-global map, so process restart clears pending queue entries. I did not run a live Telegram restart reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor live-behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the data-at-rest/session-state boundary before any merge path; the narrow code finding should be fixed on the PR branch.

target.title: feat(queue): persist followup queues across gateway restarts
target.body:
## Problem

When the gateway process restarts (npm update, crash, systemd restart), any
messages that arrived while an agent turn was in progress are silently lost.
The followup queue lives only in process memory ŌĆö there is no recovery path.

This is particularly noticeable on channels that serialize messages (e.g.
Telegram in queue mode): a message received during an active turn is queued
for delivery, then gone if the gateway restarts before that turn completes.

## Solution

Add a JSON persistence layer for the `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` global map.

### New file: `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/persist.ts`

- `persistFollowupQueues()` ŌĆö serializes all non-empty queue entries to
  `<stateDir>/live-chat-followup-queues.json`. Respects `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`
  via the existing `resolveStateDir()` utility. State file is deleted when all
  queues drain to empty.
- `restoreFollowupQueues()` ŌĆö reads the state file at module init and repopulates
  `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` before any enqueue or drain fires.
- `PersistedFollowupRun` ŌĆö a `Pick<FollowupRun, ...>` type that explicitly
  excludes runtime-only, non-serializable fields: `abortSignal`,
  `currentTurnContext`, `deliveryCorrelations`, `queuedLifecycle`. These are
  transient and must not survive a restart.

### Mutation hooks

`persistFollowupQueues()` is called after every state-changing operation:

| Site | File |
|---|---|
| `enqueueFollowupRun` ŌĆö after `items.push` | `enqueue.ts` |
| Drain `finally` block ŌĆö both empty-queue delete and reschedule branches | `drain.ts` |
| `clearFollowupQueue` ŌĆö after `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES.delete` | `state.ts` |
| `refreshQueuedFollowupSession` ŌĆö after the run-rewrite loop | `state.ts` |

### Tests

`persist.test.ts` covers:
- State file written when queue has items
- State file deleted when all queues are empty
- Full round-trip: enqueue ŌåÆ persist ŌåÆ simulated restart ŌåÆ restore ŌåÆ verify prompt + routing fields
- Runtime-only `abortSignal` field not present after restore
- No-op when state file does not exist
- Malformed entries (non-array items, null key) are skipped

## Notes

- The `draining: false` invariant is enforced on restore ŌĆö any in-flight drain
  that was interrupted is restarted by the normal drain scheduler on next enqueue.
- `summarySources` (contains `FollowupRun` objects, not serializable) is reset to
  `[]` on restore. `summaryLines` (plain strings) is persisted and restored.
- No schema migration needed for v1; unrecognized fields are ignored on restore.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Queued followup messages (received while an agent turn is active) now survive gateway restarts. Before this patch a process restart discarded all pending items silently; after it they are written to `~/.openclaw/state/live-chat-followup-queues.json` and re-delivered once the drain callback is registered on startup.
- Real environment tested: Local dev, Node 22, Linux, openclaw source checkout (`jewseppi/openclaw` `feat/queue-followup-persistence`). Module loaded directly via `node --import tsx` ŌĆö no test runner involved.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` against the live queue module to exercise the full persist ŌåÆ simulated-restart ŌåÆ restore cycle against a real temp directory.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from `node --import tsx` (live module, real filesystem I/O, no mocks):
  ```
  Step 1: persistFollowupQueues()
  State file: /tmp/openclaw-persist-proof-SdQ5bE/live-chat-followup-queues.json
  {
    "version": 1,
    "updatedAt": 1778974677687,
    "entries": [
      [
        "agent:main:dm:telegram-proof",
        {
          "items": [
            {
              "prompt": "did the gateway restart survive?",
              "enqueuedAt": 1778974677687,
              "originatingChannel": "telegram",
              "originatingTo": "99999",
              "run": {
                "agentId": "main",
                "agentDir": "/tmp/agent",
                "sessionId": "sess-proof",
                "sessionFile": "/tmp/sess.jsonl",
                "workspaceDir": "/tmp/ws",
                "provider": "anthropic",
                "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
                "timeoutMs": 30000,
                "blockReplyBreak": "message_end"
              }
            }
          ],
          "mode": "steer",
          "debounceMs": 500,
          "cap": 20,
          "dropPolicy": "summarize",
          "droppedCount": 0,
          "summaryLines": []
        }
      ]
    ]
  }

  Step 2: simulate restart ŌĆö delete in-memory queue
    FOLLOWUP_QUEUES.get(KEY): undefined

  Step 3: restoreFollowupQueues()
    restored.items.length: 1
    restored.items[0].prompt: did the gateway restart survive?
    restored.items[0].originatingChannel: telegram
    restored.draining: false
    peekRestoredPendingDrainKeys().has(KEY): true

  Pass: queue survives a simulated process restart.
  ```
- Observed result after fix: 1 queued Telegram prompt survived the in-process restart simulation. `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` was cleared to undefined, `restoreFollowupQueues()` re-populated it from the state file on disk, `draining` reset to `false`, and the key was registered in `restoredPendingDrainKeys` so drain scheduling fires when the callback is next registered. State file written and read by `node` against a real temp dir ŌĆö no mocks, no vitest.
- What was not tested: Full live TelegramŌåÆgatewayŌåÆkillŌåÆrestartŌåÆre-delivery end-to-end (requires a real inbound message arriving during an active agent turn followed by a real process restart).

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: jewseppi

  that was interrupted is restarted by the normal drain scheduler on next enqueue.
- `summarySources` (contains `FollowupRun` objects, not serializable) is reset to
  `[]` on restore. `summaryLines` (plain strings) is persisted and restored.
- No schema migration needed for v1; unrecognized fields are ignored on restore.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Queued followup messages (received while an agent turn is active) now survive gateway restarts. Before this patch a process restart discarded all pending items silently; after it they are written to `~/.openclaw/state/live-chat-followup-queues.json` and re-delivered once the drain callback is registered on startup.
- Real environment tested: Local dev, Node 22, Linux, openclaw source checkout (`jewseppi/openclaw` `feat/queue-followup-persistence`). Module loaded directly via `node --import tsx` ŌĆö no test runner involved.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` against the live queue module to exercise the full persist ŌåÆ simulated-restart ŌåÆ restore cycle against a real temp directory.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from `node --import tsx` (live module, real filesystem I/O, no mocks):
  ```
  Step 1: persistFollowupQueues()
  State file: /tmp/openclaw-persist-proof-SdQ5bE/live-chat-followup-queues.json
  {
    "version": 1,
    "updatedAt": 1778974677687,
    "entries": [
      [
        "agent:main:dm:telegram-proof",
        {
          "items": [
            {
              "prompt": "did the gateway restart survive?",
              "enqueuedAt": 1778974677687,
              "originatingChannel": "telegram",
              "originatingTo": "99999",
              "run": {
                "agentId": "main",
                "agentDir": "/tmp/agent",
                "sessionId": "sess-proof",
                "sessionFile": "/tmp/sess.jsonl",
                "workspaceDir": "/tmp/ws",
                "provider": "anthropic",
                "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
                "timeoutMs": 30000,
                "blockReplyBreak": "message_end"
              }
            }
          ],
          "mode": "steer",
          "debounceMs": 500,
          "cap": 20,
          "dropPolicy": "summarize",
          "droppedCount": 0,
          "summaryLines": []
        }
      ]
    ]
  }

  Step 2: simulate restart ŌĆö delete in-memory queue
    FOLLOWUP_QUEUES.get(KEY): undefined

  Step 3: restoreFollowupQueues()
    restored.items.length: 1
    restored.items[0].prompt: did the gateway restart survive?
    restored.items[0].originatingChannel: telegram
    restored.draining: false
    peekRestoredPendingDrainKeys().has(KEY): true

  Pass: queue survives a simulated process restart.
  ```
- Observed result after fix: 1 queued Telegram prompt survived the in-process restart simulation. `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` was cleared to undefined, `restoreFollowupQueues()` re-populated it from the state file on disk, `draining` reset to `false`, and the key was registered in `restoredPendingDrainKeys` so drain scheduling fires when the callback is next registered. State file written and read by `node` against a real temp dir ŌĆö no mocks, no vitest.
- What was not tested: Full live TelegramŌåÆgatewayŌåÆkillŌåÆrestartŌåÆre-delivery end-to-end (requires a real inbound message arriving during an active agent turn followed by a real process restart).

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: feat(queue): persist followup queues across gateway restarts
target.body:
## Problem

When the gateway process restarts (npm update, crash, systemd restart), any
messages that arrived while an agent turn was in progress are silently lost.
The followup queue lives only in process memory ŌĆö there is no recovery path.

This is particularly noticeable on channels that serialize messages (e.g.
Telegram in queue mode): a message received during an active turn is queued
for delivery, then gone if the gateway restarts before that turn completes.

## Solution

Add a JSON persistence layer for the `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` global map.

### New file: `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/persist.ts`

- `persistFollowupQueues()` ŌĆö serializes all non-empty queue entries to
  `<stateDir>/live-chat-followup-queues.json`. Respects `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`
  via the existing `resolveStateDir()` utility. State file is deleted when all
  queues drain to empty.
- `restoreFollowupQueues()` ŌĆö reads the state file at module init and repopulates
  `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` before any enqueue or drain fires.
- `PersistedFollowupRun` ŌĆö a `Pick<FollowupRun, ...>` type that explicitly
  excludes runtime-only, non-serializable fields: `abortSignal`,
  `currentTurnContext`, `deliveryCorrelations`, `queuedLifecycle`. These are
  transient and must not survive a restart.

### Mutation hooks

`persistFollowupQueues()` is called after every state-changing operation:

| Site | File |
|---|---|
| `enqueueFollowupRun` ŌĆö after `items.push` | `enqueue.ts` |
| Drain `finally` block ŌĆö both empty-queue delete and reschedule branches | `drain.ts` |
| `clearFollowupQueue` ŌĆö after `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES.delete` | `state.ts` |
| `refreshQueuedFollowupSession` ŌĆö after the run-rewrite loop | `state.ts` |

### Tests

`persist.test.ts` covers:
- State file written when queue has items
- State file deleted when all queues are empty
- Full round-trip: enqueue ŌåÆ persist ŌåÆ simulated restart ŌåÆ restore ŌåÆ verify prompt + routing fields
- Runtime-only `abortSignal` field not present after restore
- No-op when state file does not exist
- Malformed entries (non-array items, null key) are skipped

## Notes

- The `draining: false` invariant is enforced on restore ŌĆö any in-flight drain
  that was interrupted is restarted by the normal drain scheduler on next enqueue.
- `summarySources` (contains `FollowupRun` objects, not serializable) is reset to
  `[]` on restore. `summaryLines` (plain strings) is persisted and restored.
- No schema migration needed for v1; unrecognized fields are ignored on restore.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Queued followup messages (received while an agent turn is active) now survive gateway restarts. Before this patch a process restart discarded all pending items silently; after it they are written to `~/.openclaw/state/live-chat-followup-queues.json` and re-delivered once the drain callback is registered on startup.
- Real environment tested: Local dev, Node 22, Linux, openclaw source checkout (`jewseppi/openclaw` `feat/queue-followup-persistence`). Module loaded directly via `node --import tsx` ŌĆö no test runner involved.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` against the live queue module to exercise the full persist ŌåÆ simulated-restart ŌåÆ restore cycle against a real temp directory.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from `node --import tsx` (live module, real filesystem I/O, no mocks):
  ```
  Step 1: persistFollowupQueues()
  State file: /tmp/openclaw-persist-proof-SdQ5bE/live-chat-followup-queues.json
  {
    "version": 1,
    "updatedAt": 1778974677687,
    "entries": [
      [
        "agent:main:dm:telegram-proof",
        {
          "items": [
            {
              "prompt": "did the gateway restart survive?",
              "enqueuedAt": 1778974677687,
              "originatingChannel": "telegram",
              "originatingTo": "99999",
              "run": {
                "agentId": "main",
                "agentDir": "/tmp/agent",
                "sessionId": "sess-proof",
                "sessionFile": "/tmp/sess.jsonl",
                "workspaceDir": "/tmp/ws",
                "provider": "anthropic",
                "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
                "timeoutMs": 30000,
                "blockReplyBreak": "message_end"
              }
            }
          ],
          "mode": "steer",
          "debounceMs": 500,
          "cap": 20,
          "dropPolicy": "summarize",
          "droppedCount": 0,
          "summaryLines": []
        }
      ]
    ]
  }

  Step 2: simulate restart ŌĆö delete in-memory queue
    FOLLOWUP_QUEUES.get(KEY): undefined

  Step 3: restoreFollowupQueues()
    restored.items.length: 1
    restored.items[0].prompt: did the gateway restart survive?
    restored.items[0].originatingChannel: telegram
    restored.draining: false
    peekRestoredPendingDrainKeys().has(KEY): true

  Pass: queue survives a simulated process restart.
  ```
- Observed result after fix: 1 queued Telegram prompt survived the in-process restart simulation. `FOLLOWUP_QUEUES` was cleared to undefined, `restoreFollowupQueues()` re-populated it from the state file on disk, `draining` reset to `false`, and the key was registered in `restoredPendingDrainKeys` so drain scheduling fires when the callback is next registered. State file written and read by `node` against a real temp dir ŌĆö no mocks, no vitest.
- What was not tested: Full live TelegramŌåÆgatewayŌåÆkillŌåÆrestartŌåÆre-delivery end-to-end (requires a real inbound message arriving during an active agent turn followed by a real process restart).

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
